basic_details.job_title: PPSC Punjab Civil Services Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 26 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Punjab Public Service Commission (PPSC) is recruiting 26 Punjab Civil Services (Executive Branch) posts including Register A-II, Register-C with Graduate pass qualification, last date 30 June 2026.

application_fee.details: Important Notes on Fee: | Fee must be paid through online mode ONLY (Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, UPI, NEFT/RTGS). Fee once paid is non-refundable. No change/edit will be allowed after fee submission. Candidates claiming fee concession must submit a self-attested copy of the relevant certificate along with the hard copy of the application form. Failure to submit this certificate will result in forfeiture of fee concession.

qualification.details: Register A-II is open to members of Group A and Group B services of Punjab Government who are holding ministerial appointments not below the level of Senior Assistants and serving in connection with the affairs of the State of Punjab. | Important: Employees of Universities, Boards, Societies, PUDA, GMADA, PSPCL, PSTCL, Corporations etc. are NOT eligible. | Register-C is open to members of Group A and Group B services of Punjab Government who are NOT covered under any of the following categories: | Register A-I: District Revenue Officers / Tehsildars / Naib Tehsildars Register A-II: Officers/officials holding "Ministerial appointments" Register A-III: Block Development & Panchayat Officers / District Development & Panchayat Officers / Assistant Excise & Taxation Commissioner / Excise & Taxation Officers | Important: Employees of Universities, Boards, Societies, PUDA, GMADA, PSPCL, PSTCL, Corporations etc. are NOT eligible. | Service Condition: The candidate must be a confirmed hand and must have completed 8 years of continuous service under the Punjab Government. | Age Limit: The candidate must be below 54 years of age as on 01 November 2025. | Educational Qualification: The candidate must be a Graduate of a recognized university as on 01 November 2025. | Punjabi Requirement: Proof of having passed Punjabi language up to Matriculation level or its equivalent is required. | Note on Register Selection: Candidates must carefully verify from their respective department which Register they fall under before applying. No change in the type of register will be permitted at any stage by PPSC. | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

selection_process: 3 Assessment of Service Records (ACRs/APARs) - Last 5 years 20
The selection process for both Register A-II and Register-C consists of four stages with a total of 100 marks:
Stage 1 - Screening Test: A Common Screening Test will be conducted for both Register A-II and Register-C. However

merit lists will be prepared separately for each register. Candidates scoring a minimum of 30% marks and ranking within 3 times the number of vacancies (in merit order) will be shortlisted for the interview.
Stage 2 - Assessment of Service Records (ACRs/APARs): Last 5 years ACRs will be considered. If 5-year record is not available
the preceding year ACRs will be considered.
Stage 3 - Length of Service: Maximum 15 marks. For each completed year of service beyond the mandatory 8 years
0.54 marks are awarded. Full 15 marks are awarded upon completing 28 years of service beyond the initial 8 years (i.e.
Stage 4 - Interview: 15 marks. The interview panel assesses the candidate's suitability based on personal qualities. Screening test marks are not disclosed to the interview panel.
Final Result: Prepared by adding 50% of screening test marks + ACR marks + Length of Service marks + Interview marks.

pattern_table.Details: Yes - 0.25 marks deducted per wrong answer

pattern_table.Subject: Civics
pattern_table.Key Topics: Freedom, equality, social justice, Panchayati Raj, Elections, People's Representation Act

pattern_table.Subject: Constitution of India
pattern_table.Key Topics: Basic features, provisions, Schedules, key amendments

pattern_table.Subject: Indian History
pattern_table.Key Topics: Indus Valley, Vedic age, Maurya-Gupta periods, Mughals, British rule, National Movement

pattern_table.Subject: Geography
pattern_table.Key Topics: Population, migration, human settlements, land/water/mineral resources, industries, trade

pattern_table.Subject: General Science
pattern_table.Key Topics: Matter, atom structure, acids/bases, human body, diseases, force, energy, light, sound, electricity, computers

pattern_table.Subject: Punjabi
pattern_table.Key Topics: Comprehension, English to Punjabi translation, grammar, vocabulary

pattern_table.Subject: English
pattern_table.Key Topics: Comprehension, Punjabi to English translation, grammar, vocabulary

pattern_table.Subject: History of Punjab
pattern_table.Key Topics: Personalities, religious movements, Sikh rulers, British period, National movement in Punjab

pattern_table.Subject: General Knowledge
pattern_table.Key Topics: National/International events, current affairs, environmental issues

exam_pattern.details: Note: If a question is withdrawn, all candidates will be awarded 1 mark regardless of whether they attempted it or not. Answer key will be uploaded on PPSC website after the exam; candidates can raise objections within 4 days through the online link only (objections via email/post will not be entertained). | The syllabus covers the following 9 subjects with at least 10 questions from each subject:

how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must apply online through the official PPSC website before 30 June 2026.

Step 1 - Register Online: Visit www.ppsc.gov.in and click on the relevant advertisement link (Advt. No. 20263 for Register A-II / Advt. No. 20264 for Register-C). Register using your details and note your Registration Number and Password.
Step 2 - Upload Photo and Signature: Upload your color photograph and signature (file size: 10 KB to 40 KB each) in the designated section. This step is mandatory before proceeding to fee payment.

Step 3 - Fill Application Form: Enter all required details carefully including personal information, service details, educational qualifications, category, and group. Verify all details before submitting as no changes will be allowed after fee payment.

Step 4 - Pay Application Fee: Click "Pay Now" and pay the applicable fee via Credit/Debit Card, Net Banking, UPI, or NEFT/RTGS. On successful payment, download and save the payment receipt. If the transaction shows "Pending" despite payment deduction, click "Verify Status" for reconciliation.

Step 5 - Submit & Print Application: After successful fee payment, submit the application form. Take a printout of the complete application form and save the Application Number for future reference including Admit Card download.

Step 6 - Submit Hard Copy through Proper Channel: Send the printed application form along with all required documents (Annexure A, B, C and certificates mentioned at clauses 5.4.1 to 5.4.9) through the Head of Department to the Office of PPSC, Baradari Gardens, Patiala - 147001 by 30 July 2026 (5:00 PM). Applications not received through proper channel will be rejected.

faq.question: Q1. What is PPSC PCS Executive Branch Recruitment 2026 and what are Register A-II and Register-C?
faq.answer: PPSC PCS (Executive Branch) Recruitment 2026 is a departmental promotion-based recruitment conducted by the Punjab Public Service Commission for serving Punjab Government employees to be elevated to Punjab Civil Services (Executive Branch). Register A-II covers Group A and B ministerial cadre employees (holding appointments not below the level of Senior Assistants), while Register-C covers Group A and B employees who do not fall under Register A-I (Revenue Officers), Register A-II (Ministerial) or Register A-III (BDPOs/DDPOs/Excise & Taxation Officers).

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for PPSC PCS Executive Branch Register A-II and Register-C Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application form and pay the examination fee is 30 June 2026 by 11:59 PM. The last date to submit the hard copy of the application form along with all required documents through proper channel (Head of Department) to PPSC Patiala is 30 July 2026 by 5:00 PM.

faq.question: Q3. What are the eligibility conditions for PPSC PCS Executive Branch Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must fulfill all three conditions as on the cut-off date 01 November 2025: (a) be a confirmed hand with at least 8 years of continuous service under Punjab Government; (b) be below 54 years of age; and (c) hold a Graduate degree from a recognized university. For Register A-II, the candidate must be in Group A or B ministerial cadre not below Senior Assistant level. For Register-C, the candidate must be in Group A or B but not covered under Registers A-I, A-II, or A-III.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for PPSC PCS Executive Branch Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The total fee is Rs. 1500/- for General/Other category candidates (Rs. 500 application + Rs. 1000 examination fee), Rs. 750/- for SC/ST (all states) and Backward Classes of Punjab (Rs. 500 + Rs. 250), and Rs. 500/- (application charges only, no examination fee) for Ex-Servicemen of Punjab, EWS, PwD, and Lineal Descendants of Ex-Servicemen of Punjab. Fee must be paid online only and is non-refundable.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for PPSC PCS Executive Branch Register A-II and Register-C 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process carries 100 total marks with four components: Screening Test (50 marks - 50% of the 100-mark test), Length of Service (15 marks - 0.54 marks per completed year beyond 8 years), Assessment of last 5 years ACRs/APARs (20 marks - graded as Average 1, Good 2, Very Good 3, Outstanding 4 per year), and Interview (15 marks).

faq.question: Q6. When is the PPSC PCS Executive Branch Screening Test 2026 scheduled?
faq.answer: Common Screening Test for both Register A-II and Register-C is tentatively scheduled for September/October 2026. The exact date and schedule will be notified separately on the official PPSC website ppsc.gov.in. Candidates are advised to regularly check the official website for updates.

faq.question: Q7. What is the syllabus and pattern of the PPSC PCS Executive Branch Screening Test 2026?
faq.answer: The screening test consists of 100 objective-type multiple-choice questions of 100 marks in 2 hours, at graduation level, in English and Punjabi. The syllabus covers 9 subjects with at least 10 questions from each: Civics, Constitution of India, Indian History, Geography, General Science, Punjabi, English, History of Punjab, and General Knowledge. There is negative marking of 0.25 marks per wrong answer. Candidates must score at least 30% to qualify.

faq.question: Q8. Is it mandatory to submit a hard copy of the application for PPSC PCS Executive Branch Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, submission of the hard copy is absolutely mandatory. After submitting the online application and paying the fee, candidates must take a printout and send it along with Annexure-A (HOD Certificate), Annexure-B (Service Record Proforma), Annexure-C (Scrutiny Form), and other supporting documents through their Head of Department to the PPSC office by 30 July 2026. Failure to submit the hard copy will result in outright rejection of candidature.

faq.question: Q9. Can employees of Boards, Corporations, Universities and PSUs apply for PPSC PCS Executive Branch Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. Employees of Universities, Boards, Societies, PUDA, GMADA, PSPCL, PSTCL, Corporations and similar autonomous bodies are not eligible to apply for PPSC PCS Executive Branch Register A-II or Register-C posts. Only employees directly serving in connection with the affairs of the State of Punjab Government are eligible.

faq.question: Q10. What scribe and compensatory time facilities are available for PwD candidates in PPSC PCS Executive Branch Screening Test 2026?
faq.answer: PwD candidates with benchmark disability (40% or more) are entitled to compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour. Candidates with Blindness, Locomotor Disability (Both Arms Affected) or Cerebral Palsy are automatically entitled to scribe facility. Other PwD candidates may also avail scribe with a medical certificate from a Government CMO/Civil Surgeon. Candidates with less than 40% disability (Section 2(s) of RPwD Act) may also apply through a Multi-Member Medical Authority certificate. All documents including UDID certificate must be submitted to PPSC by 30 July 2026.

title: PPSC Punjab Civil Services Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 26 Posts
